title: feat: Add Podman artifact support to Go bindings and remote clients
body:
Add the Go bindings implementation necessary to support Artifacts.
Implement the tunnel interface that consumes the Artifacts Go bindings.

With this patch, users of the Podman remote clients will now be able to manage OCI artifacts via the Podman CLI and Podman machine. 

Jira: https://issues.redhat.com/browse/RUN-2714#

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Added support for managing OCI Artifacts in remote Podman clients (Mac/Windows)
Added support to the Podman Go bindings for managing OCI Artifacts
```

comments.body: [NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
For testing you should remove the `SkipIfRemote()` in test/e2e/artifact_test.go and test/e2e/artifact_mount_test.go

```
 $ podman [options] artifact add --type octet/foobar --append localhost/test/artifact1 /tmp/CI_v0QL/podman-e2e-1274195134/subtest-1132160425/p/artifacts/Mfkoclwv
           Error: append option is not compatible with ArtifactMIMEType option
```

I guess you need to make sure you only pass the type on the first request not when adding append.
No pressure, but QE is expecting this in a build next week.  If that won't happen, not a problem, but I'll need to let the know.

> No pressure, but QE is expecting this in a build next week. If that won't happen, not a problem, but I'll need to let the know.

Thanks for the heads up @TomSweeneyRedHat , I was working on event support for Artifacts at the same time as this one but I will just focus on this now. 

The only road blocks I see now are needing to add "remove all" support into the artifact API and needing to update the artifact push and pull response to include the whole error message if there is one.

@Luap99 With the remaining test failure [Podman-artifact-podman-artifact-extract-evil](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4690140989227008/html/int-remote-fedora-42-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-artifact-podman-artifact-extract-evil--1)

It's failing because when a blob has an "evil" title it should still extract if you're extracting to a file ad the evil name is ignored and the new name is used.

I don't have access to the path to test if it's a dir or file because on the server side I write directly to the tar writer.

Currently I'm already inspecting the artifact on the client side before inspecting for another reason. I could move the malicious title check to the client side in the bindings for extract. There I know if the destination is a file or not and if the blob title is "evil" and act accordingly. 

WDYT?

> @Luap99 With the remaining test failure [Podman-artifact-podman-artifact-extract-evil](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4690140989227008/html/int-remote-fedora-42-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-artifact-podman-artifact-extract-evil--1)
> 
> It's failing because when a blob has an "evil" title it should still extract if you're extracting to a file ad the evil name is ignored and the new name is used.
> 
> I don't have access to the path to test if it's a dir or file because on the server side I write directly to the tar writer.
> 
> Currently I'm already inspecting the artifact on the client side before inspecting for another reason. I could move the malicious title check to the client side in the bindings for extract. There I know if the destination is a file or not and if the blob title is "evil" and act accordingly.

We should never be sending a tar with a malicious filename IMO as we should not trust the clients to handle that. Sure we control podman-remote but the API might be used by anyone directly who will not know that.

Would it make sense to expose another API endpoint that just returns the plain file content in the body (not tar) that only works for a single file. In that case the client can check if we extract to file and call the other endpoint which should work fine? Or maybe not a full separate endpoint but only a query param?

> > @Luap99 With the remaining test failure [Podman-artifact-podman-artifact-extract-evil](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4690140989227008/html/int-remote-fedora-42-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-artifact-podman-artifact-extract-evil--1)
> > It's failing because when a blob has an "evil" title it should still extract if you're extracting to a file ad the evil name is ignored and the new name is used.
> > I don't have access to the path to test if it's a dir or file because on the server side I write directly to the tar writer.
> > Currently I'm already inspecting the artifact on the client side before inspecting for another reason. I could move the malicious title check to the client side in the bindings for extract. There I know if the destination is a file or not and if the blob title is "evil" and act accordingly.
> 
> We should never be sending a tar with a malicious filename IMO as we should not trust the clients to handle that. Sure we control podman-remote but the API might be used by anyone directly who will not know that.
> 
> Would it make sense to expose another API endpoint that just returns the plain file content in the body (not tar) that only works for a single file. In that case the client can check if we extract to file and call the other endpoint which should work fine? Or maybe not a full separate endpoint but only a query param?

Good idea, I'll go the route of adding query param. I'll default to not send artifacts with malicious titles, then in the bindings I can allow them if we writing to a file rather than a directory!

I think this current failure is unrelated:

The rebase added some new tests I need to get passing.
Restarted one failure that looks like a flake to me. One nit, code otherwise LGTM.

Seems like a flake, I don't have access to rerun the testing farm jobs sadly, could someone please rerun :)
